cit.basis: Flat 30% CIT on resident companies and non-resident branches (Income Tax Act, Cap 340).
cit.note: Administered by the Uganda Revenue Authority (URA). Branch repatriated-income tax, mining/petroleum variable rates, and exemption regimes are out of scope for this SME estimator.

vat.note: 18% standard VAT rate (VAT Act, Cap 349); registration threshold UGX 150 million annual turnover; zero-rated and exempt supplies apply to specific goods and services.

presumptive_turnover_tax.note: Small-business presumptive regime: resident taxpayers with annual turnover up to UGX 150 million may pay a turnover-based presumptive tax (banded fixed amounts / rates) instead of standard CIT.

digital_services_tax.note: 5% Digital Services Tax (DST) on income derived by non-residents from providing digital services to consumers in Uganda.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars (Uganda fiscal year runs July-June) and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Presumptive regime eligibility, branch repatriation tax, WHT, relief, filing, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
